title: Have 1 Seat Plan

posts.author: JackSy12 (Jack Symons)
posts.cookedHtml:
I am a small business and I don’t have a team nor require 3 Seats, but I do need the automations.
Offering a 1 seat plan would allow people like myself to continue to use and pay for the service

Currently I’m having to use the free plan although I’d like to pay for Standard, and I know a handful of other small business owners in the same spot.

My options now are to look for an alternative to Monday, or stick with the free plan which doesn’t cover what I need

Paying for 3 seats when only one is needed is an unnecessary expense

posts.author: Elve (Elve)
posts.cookedHtml: I have the same question as a sole proprietor. It doesn’t offer me the possibility of creating a sole user account.

posts.author: testuser90 (Habeeb  E Sadeed)
posts.cookedHtml: One seat is also needed for me. 3 seat price is expensive.

posts.author: RachelS11 (Rachel Schelle)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree with this 101%. The layout options, colors, automations, field options… really everything about Monday.com makes my workaholic, ADHD, overwhelmed mom self feel at ease - or at least as good as I any tool that helps organize my constant running inner-dialog can. Monday.com keeps me from getting burnt out and overwhelmed in a way no other task list app has. I don’t need to share my account with anyone. It’s really hard to justify the cost of 3 seats in my budget when it’s really just for me. I think you’d find there’s more users out there if there was an option that allowed the standard features priced for 1 seat (or 2) seats. I know I’d tell all my neurodivergent networks about it!

posts.author: samrikken (Sam)
posts.cookedHtml:
Absolutely agree with you. I’m also a solo business owner and have been using Monday.com for over 6 years—it’s been my go-to for managing everything.

But like you said, the 3-seat minimum makes the plan too expensive for just one person. I’d love to keep using the more advanced features, but the cost just doesn’t make sense when I only need one seat.

Really hope the Monday team considers a 1-seat plan. There are so many freelancers and small one-person businesses who’d benefit from it.
